status: DEV — CLINICIAN-ATTESTED (KL, MED0001857758, 2026-07-15 via signed worksheet); NOT patient-facing; NOT a clinical source until FL-30 Step 5 staging validation + regulatory (TGA) sign-off. AU doses are the clinician's own verbatim APF22 Section D text (facts, cited); the agent segmented and labelled for display and wrote no dose (schema substring bar). Tier A + amoxicillin.

attestation.recorded_by: claude-fable-5 (agent, on clinician KL explicit sign-off)
attestation.statement: Registered medical practitioner Kenneth Lee (MED0001857758) reviewed the R-47a attestation worksheet (eval/pharmacology/signoff/dose-guidance-worksheet-KL-2026-07-15.md) and marked ALL 11 records Attest, 0 Amend, 0 Reject. Each record was presented with: his own verbatim APF22 source statement, every segmented dose line with indication/route/dosing-basis/plausibility, and every US/EU comparator label dose VERBATIM with its authorisation status — including the carbamazepine order-of-magnitude flag (AU max 2 g vs US initial 200 mg) and metformin's WITHDRAWN US comparator. CLINICAL sign-off only; regulatory (TGA) NOT given; dataset remains -dev and non-patient-facing.
attestation.scope: pharm-dose-guidance dev v0.1.0 — 11 records (Tier A + amoxicillin), all review_status 'approved'. AU doses only; paediatric rows deliberately excluded (the paediatric hard limit is unchanged and its plan is parked). Attested against the R-47a worksheet, retained as the medicolegal artifact.

reseal_history.reason: C2d clinician attestation applied: KL (MED0001857758) marked all 11 records Attest / 0 Amend / 0 Reject on the R-47a worksheet (eval/pharmacology/signoff/dose-guidance-worksheet-KL-2026-07-15.md), and the application set provenance.reviewed_by/review_status on every record. The seal was computed at authoring time when the records were draft, so the sign-off invalidated it -- exactly the R-46 mechanism, this time surfaced by the assertion R-46 added rather than decaying silently. No clinical content changed: only the two provenance review fields the attestation is supposed to change.

records.context: adult — APF22 Section D common dosage range
records.source_statement: Rheumatology/dermatology: 5–25 mg once weekly (may be taken in three divided doses at 0, 12 and 24 hours). Antineoplastic chemotherapy: See approved product information and specialist protocols.

dose_lines.statement: 5–25 mg once weekly (may be taken in three divided doses at 0, 12 and 24 hours).
dose_lines.plausibility_note: AU dose (max 25 mg) is within 10x of the closest foreign label (max 7.5 mg, 3.3x). No order-of-magnitude entry error detected. This says NOTHING about clinical congruence — jurisdictions legitimately differ.

dose_lines.statement: See approved product information and specialist protocols.
dose_lines.plausibility_note: AU dose not comparable: no mass amount found. No plausibility claim is made — this is NOT an all-clear.

records.safe_dose_range: Rheumatology/dermatology: 5–25 mg once weekly (may be taken in three divided doses at 0, 12 and 24 hours). Antineoplastic chemotherapy: See approved product information and specialist protocols.

comparators.dose_statement: The recommended starting dosage of JYLAMVO is 7.5 mg orally once weekly with escalation to achieve optimal response. Dosages of more than 20 mg once weekly result in an increased risk of serious adverse reactions, including myelosuppression.
The recommended initial dose is 7.5 mg (3.75 ml) methotrexate once weekly.

records.context: adult — APF22 Section D common dosage range
records.source_statement: Epilepsy: Initially 100 mg twice daily; increase gradually until optimum response achieved. Usual dose 400–1,200 mg daily in two or more doses. Up to 2 g daily may be required.

dose_lines.statement: Initially 100 mg twice daily; increase gradually until optimum response achieved. Usual dose 400–1,200 mg daily in two or more doses. Up to 2 g daily may be required.
dose_lines.plausibility_note: AU dose (max 2000 mg) differs from the US label (max 200 mg) by 10.0x — at or beyond the 10x threshold. This is an ORDER-OF-MAGNITUDE flag for a human (a misplaced zero looks exactly like this), NOT a judgement that the AU dose is wrong and NOT a block. Confirm the entry against the source before attesting.

records.safe_dose_range: Epilepsy: Initially 100 mg twice daily; increase gradually until optimum response achieved. Usual dose 400–1,200 mg daily in two or more doses. Up to 2 g daily may be required.

comparators.dose_statement: The recommended initial dose of EQUETRO is 200 mg administered twice daily. The dose may be increased by 200 mg per day to achieve optimal clinical response.

records.context: adult — APF22 Section D common dosage range
records.source_statement: Immediate-release tablet, 500–1,000 mg daily in one or two doses; maximum 3,000 mg daily in three doses. Controlled-release tablet, 500–2,000 mg once daily.

records.safe_dose_range: Immediate-release tablet, 500–1,000 mg daily in one or two doses; maximum 3,000 mg daily in three doses. Controlled-release tablet, 500–2,000 mg once daily.

comparators.dose_statement: The recommended starting dose of GLUMETZA is 500 mg orally once daily with the evening meal. Increase the dose in increments of 500 mg every 1 to 2 weeks on the basis of glycemic control and tolerability, up to a maximum of 2,000 mg once daily with the evening meal.

records.context: adult — APF22 Section D common dosage range
records.source_statement: Ulcerative colitis: 2–4 g daily in three or four divided doses. Rheumatoid arthritis: Initially 500 mg daily, increasing by 500 mg each week to 2–3 g daily in divided doses.

dose_lines.statement: 2–4 g daily in three or four divided doses.
dose_lines.plausibility_note: AU dose (max 4000 mg) is within 10x of the closest foreign label (max 4000 mg, 1.0x). No order-of-magnitude entry error detected. This says NOTHING about clinical congruence — jurisdictions legitimately differ.

dose_lines.statement: Initially 500 mg daily, increasing by 500 mg each week to 2–3 g daily in divided doses.

records.safe_dose_range: Ulcerative colitis: 2–4 g daily in three or four divided doses. Rheumatoid arthritis: Initially 500 mg daily, increasing by 500 mg each week to 2–3 g daily in divided doses.

comparators.dose_statement: Adults: 3 to 4 g daily in evenly divided doses with dosage intervals not exceeding eight hours. It may be advisable to initiate therapy with a lower dosage, e.g., 1 to 2 g daily, to reduce possible gastrointestinal intolerance.

records.context: adult — APF22 Section D common dosage range
records.source_statement: Anticonvulsant: Oral, initially 4–5 mg/kg daily in two or three doses. Adjust dosage according to plasma levels; usual maintenance dose 200–500 mg daily. Maximum daily dose 600 mg. Status epilepticus: IV, 15–20 mg/kg.

dose_lines.statement: Oral, initially 4–5 mg/kg daily in two or three doses. Adjust dosage according to plasma levels; usual maintenance dose 200–500 mg daily. Maximum daily dose 600 mg.
dose_lines.plausibility_note: AU dose (max 600 mg) is within 10x of the closest foreign label (max 125 mg, 4.8x). No order-of-magnitude entry error detected. This says NOTHING about clinical congruence — jurisdictions legitimately differ.
AU dose not comparable: weight- or BSA-based only (mg/kg, mg/m²) — a different scale, never compared to a flat mg dose. No plausibility claim is made — this is NOT an all-clear.

records.safe_dose_range: Anticonvulsant: Oral, initially 4–5 mg/kg daily in two or three doses. Adjust dosage according to plasma levels; usual maintenance dose 200–500 mg daily. Maximum daily dose 600 mg. Status epilepticus: IV, 15–20 mg/kg.

comparators.dose_statement: The recommended starting dosage for adult patients who have received no previous treatment is 5 mL (125 mg/5 mL), or one teaspoonful, by mouth three times daily. Adjust the dosage to suit individual requirements, up to a maximum of 25 mL daily.

records.safe_dose_range: 2.5 mg twice daily; initial dose taken 12–24 hours after surgery.

comparators.dose_statement: The recommended dose of ELIQUIS for most patients is 5 mg taken orally twice daily. The recommended dose of ELIQUIS is 2.5 mg twice daily in patients with at least two of the following characteristics: age greater than or equal to 80 years, body weight less than or equal to 60 kg, serum creatinine greater than or equal to 1.5 mg/dL

The recommended dose of apixaban is 5 mg taken orally twice daily. Dose reduction: The recommended dose of apixaban is 2.5 mg taken orally twice daily in patients with NVAF and at least two of the following characteristics: age >= 80 years, body weight <= 60 kg, or serum creatinine >= 1.5 mg/dL (133 micromole/L).

comparators.dose_statement: For patients with creatinine clearance (CrCl) > 30 mL/min, the recommended dosage of PRADAXA Capsules is 150 mg taken orally, twice daily. For patients with severe renal impairment (CrCl 15-30 mL/min), the recommended dosage of PRADAXA Capsules is 75 mg twice daily
300 mg dabigatran etexilate taken as one 150 mg capsule twice daily
